seed_problem: job-shop with d-dimensional job profiles and squared-Euclidean changeover setup; minimize makespan + total setup
mutation: composite: couple a makespan partition across identical parallel stages with a per-stage squared-Euclidean routing (setup) problem, and charge setup twice on the critical stage (nonlinear coupling) so the winning strategy must balance load AND order acts geometrically
title: Aurora Festival: Stage Reconfiguration Scheduling
strategies: trivial one-stage: all acts on stage 1 in input order (the judge baseline)
LPT load balancer ignoring geometry: assign longest-runtime acts to least-loaded stage; balances makespan but scrambles rig clusters and pays huge setup (the SPT/LPT trap)
nearest-neighbor profile tour cut into M runtime-balanced contiguous segments: balances makespan while keeping geometrically-similar acts adjacent (low setup)
cluster acts in profile space, assign whole clusters to stages to balance load, then order each stage by a local nearest-neighbor / 2-opt path
coupled incremental greedy: insert each act into the (stage, position) minimizing the marginal increase of makespan + setup, then local-search reassign
